title: Why Did ADT Inc. (ADT) Stock Move Down -12.59% Today?
author: tickeron_community
selftext:
# Key Takeaways

* [ADT](https://tickeron.com/ticker/ADT/) shares dropped sharply — falling as much as 13.4% intraday and hitting a new 52-week low of $6.65 — after reporting Q4 2025 results before the Monday open
* Fourth-quarter revenue and guidance both missed analyst expectations, overshadowing an earnings-per-share beat
* Full-year 2026 revenue guidance of $5.1 billion trailed the $5.3 billion consensus by approximately 4%, signaling flat growth ahead
* 2026 EPS guidance of $0.89 came in just below the $0.90 analyst consensus, with management citing focus on cash flow stabilization and share buybacks over growth acceleration
* Morgan Stanley cut its price target on [ADT](https://tickeron.com/ticker/ADT/) from $9.50 to $9.00, maintaining an "equal weight" rating
* Traders are watching whether the stock can hold above its 52-week low of $6.65 and whether forward guidance improves as the Origin AI integration progresses

# Opening Summary

ADT Inc. ([ADT](https://tickeron.com/ticker/ADT/)), a Boca Raton, Florida-based provider of residential and small-business security monitoring, smart home automation, and interactive services, saw its shares plunge approximately 12.59% on Monday, March 2, 2026. The stock's previous close was $8.025, and shares slid to around $7.015 during the session — touching an intraday low of $6.65, a fresh 52-week bottom. The selloff was triggered by the company's pre-market release of fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 earnings results, which delivered a headline EPS beat but a notable revenue shortfall and a 2026 guidance outlook that disappointed Wall Street.

# Earnings Results: EPS Beat Overshadowed by Revenue Miss

[ADT](https://tickeron.com/ticker/ADT/) reported Q4 2025 adjusted EPS of $0.23, beating the $0.20 analyst consensus by $0.03 — a 15% positive surprise. The company also posted full-year 2025 adjusted EPS of $0.89, up 19% year-over-year, and full-year adjusted free cash flow of $863 million, reflecting a 16% gain. These were credible operational achievements.

However, fourth-quarter revenue came in below analyst expectations, and profit dropped compared to the same period in the prior year. Net income for Q4 fell on a year-over-year basis, and the quarterly adjusted free cash flow declined 31% to $154 million — a data point that caught investors' attention and suggested seasonal or structural cash generation pressure heading into 2026.

# Weak 2026 Guidance: The Core Catalyst

The most damaging element of Monday's release was the company's forward outlook. [ADT](https://tickeron.com/ticker/ADT/) issued full-year 2026 revenue guidance of $5.1 billion, approximately 4% below the $5.3 billion Wall Street consensus. EPS guidance of $0.89 also came in a tick below the $0.90 consensus estimate. Management framed 2026 as a year of "stabilizing cash flow" and executing on share repurchases — language markets read as a signal that meaningful top-line acceleration is not expected in the near term.

The guidance miss was compounded by the context: [ADT](https://tickeron.com/ticker/ADT/) had just closed a $170 million acquisition of Origin AI in February 2026 and entered a long-term technology licensing agreement with European security leader Verisure. Investors had anticipated these moves might support a more optimistic outlook. Instead, the company signaled flat revenue and earnings growth, raising questions about the pace of return on its AI and technology investments.

# Analyst Reactions and Price Target Cuts

Morgan Stanley had already reduced its price target on [ADT](https://tickeron.com/ticker/ADT/) from $9.50 to $9.00 in December 2025, with an "equal weight" rating. Following Monday's results, the consensus among the six analysts covering the stock remains "Hold," with an average price target of $9.25 — still significantly above the current trading level, though targets have drifted lower in recent months.

Zacks Research upgraded [ADT](https://tickeron.com/ticker/ADT/) from "strong sell" to "hold" in February 2026, and Wall Street Zen downgraded it from "buy" to "hold" in November 2025 — a pattern that reflects a broadly cautious institutional view even before Monday's guidance disappointment. No analyst upgrades were reported in the immediate aftermath of the earnings release.

# Market Context and Trading Activity

[ADT](https://tickeron.com/ticker/ADT/) volume of approximately 4.9 million shares through mid-session was running well below the 20-day average of roughly 8.9 million, suggesting the drop was driven more by seller urgency and thin buying interest than by broad institutional liquidation. The stock breached both its 50-day moving average of $8.04 and its 200-day moving average of $8.35 with force, placing the technical structure in a clearly bearish configuration.

The 52-week low of $6.65 touched intraday represents the stock's weakest print in over a year. Broader indices traded in a mixed-to-lower range on Monday, but [ADT](https://tickeron.com/ticker/ADT/)'s decline was decisively stock-specific and far outpaced any sector-level weakness in security and automation services peers.

# Trending AI Robots

Tickeron's [Trending AI Robots](https://tickeron.com/bot-trading/trending-robots/) page highlights the platform's best-performing AI trading bots under current live market conditions. Tickeron operates hundreds of automated bots across thousands of tickers, spanning strategies such as momentum, swing trading, and mean reversion — each evaluated on distinct timeframes and performance metrics including win rate, return, and drawdown. For traders navigating volatile sessions like today's earnings-driven moves, exploring algorithmically validated strategies through Tickeron's Trending AI Robots can offer a disciplined, data-driven complement to fundamental analysis.

# What Comes Next for ADT

The key near-term watchpoint for [ADT](https://tickeron.com/ticker/ADT/) is whether management can articulate a credible path to revenue reacceleration when it next speaks to investors. The company's multi-year framework targets 5% annual revenue growth and 10% adjusted EPS growth — but the 2026 guidance implies neither target will be met in the immediate term.

The integration of Origin AI into the ADT+ platform and the rollout of new ambient sensing and safety features will be closely tracked as leading indicators of whether the company's AI strategy can generate tangible subscriber growth and improved monetization. The $1.5 billion share repurchase authorization provides a near-term technical floor for the stock, but its effectiveness depends on execution timing and pace. Macro headwinds including consumer spending sensitivity and competitive pressure in smart home security from large technology platforms remain ongoing considerations for the stock's recovery trajectory.
